subtitle: The Lion of Punjab Stands Against a Voiceless Commission

panels.shot: low-angle heroic mid-shot framed against the skyline of Lahore with a faint outline of a marching crowd behind
panels.action: Lala Lajpat Rai stands firm in a white kurta and shawl, chest slightly forward, eyes blazing with calm resolve, as a misty vision of protesting Indians and looming British officials forms behind him over the Lahore streets.

dialogue.text: Lahore, 1928: as a foreign commission arrives without Indian voice, Lala Lajpat Rai steps forth to face an empire with disciplined defiance.

i18n.hi: लाहौर, 1928: बिना भारतीयों वाला विदेशी कमीशन आते ही, लाला लाजपत राय अनुशासनभरी अवहेलना लेकर साम्राज्य से भिड़ने निकले।

panels.shot: wide aerial view of Lahore cityscape at dawn
panels.action: The old city of Lahore lies under a pale dawn, its domes, minarets and rooftops shrouded in light fog as thin smoke rises from early morning hearths and the colonial clocktower stands apart across the river.

dialogue.text: The sun rose over Lahore, over winding bazaars and quiet courtyards, over a city that had seen Mughals, Sikhs, and now the firm hand of the British Raj.

i18n.hi: सूरज लाहौर पर उगा—संकरी गलियों, चुप आँगनों, उस शहर पर जिसने मुग़ल, सिक्ख और अब सख़्त अंग्रेज़ हुकूमत देखी थी।

panels.shot: medium shot of a British newspaper on a desk, with official hands nearby
panels.action: On a wooden desk in a colonial office, a neatly folded English newspaper lies open to a headline about the Simon Commission, while unseen officials in stiff suits gesture over maps and documents.

dialogue.text: In London, a commission was formed to examine India’s future. Not a single Indian was asked to sit at its table.

i18n.hi: लंदन में भारत के भविष्य पर एक कमीशन बना, उसकी मेज़ पर एक भी भारतीय कुर्सी नहीं थी।

panels.shot: close-up of an Indian-language newspaper being unfolded by brown hands
panels.action: Brown, work-worn hands unfold an Indian newspaper on a charpoy; a bold headline in Gurmukhi and Urdu announces the Simon Commission’s arrival and calls from leaders for protest.

dialogue.text: In Lahore, the news ran through homes, shops, and schools. A foreign group, deciding laws for millions, without one Indian voice among them.

i18n.hi: लाहौर में खबर घर–घर, बाज़ार–बाज़ार, स्कूल–स्कूल दौड़ी—विदेशी टोली, करोड़ों पर क़ानून थोपने चली, पर एक भी भारतीय आवाज़ नहीं।

panels.shot: intimate interior mid-shot in a modest study, side-lit
panels.action: Inside a modest study lined with books and a spinning wheel in the corner, Lala Lajpat Rai sits straight-backed at a desk, reading the paper, his brows knit, his hand resting on a pen beside scattered notes.

dialogue.text: Lala Lajpat Rai, the Lion of Punjab, read the notices in silence. He had returned from exile and prison; the struggle had carved lines into his face, but not into his courage.

i18n.hi: लाला लाजपत राय, पंजाब का शेर, चुपचाप इश्तहार पढ़ते रहे। जेल और देश–निकाला काट चुके थे; चेहरे पर लकीरें थीं, दिल पर नहीं।

panels.shot: medium close-up on his face, thoughtful, candlelight flickering
panels.action: A close view shows Lajpat Rai’s weathered face in warm lamplight, eyes thoughtful yet fiery, as shadows from the flame dance over his features and the edges of framed portraits of national leaders on the wall.

dialogue.speaker: lala_lajpat_rai
dialogue.text: If we accept a voiceless commission, what dignity of a nation remains?

i18n.hi: अगर हम गूँगा कमीशन मान लें, तो क़ौम की इज़्ज़त कहाँ बचेगी?

panels.shot: medium shot in a gathering hall with leaders around a table
panels.action: In a crowded hall with high ceilings and whirring fans, Lajpat Rai stands at the head of a wooden table, addressing a circle of local leaders seated on benches, some taking notes, others listening intently.

dialogue.text: Across India, parties and leaders agreed to boycott the Simon Commission. In Lahore, Lajpat Rai called for protest, not chaos: a line of discipline between outrage and honour.

i18n.hi: पूरे देश में दल और नेता साइमन कमीशन के बहिष्कार पर एकजुट हुए। लाहौर में लाजपत राय ने विरोध बुलाया, हंगामा नहीं—ग़ुस्से और सम्मान के बीच सीधी रेखा।

panels.shot: three-quarter shot from behind him as he addresses volunteers
panels.action: Seen from behind, Lajpat Rai faces a group of young volunteers standing in rows, their simple clothes neat, as he lifts one hand gently, emphasizing order and restraint.

dialogue.speaker: lala_lajpat_rai
dialogue.text: We will show strength in restraint. No stone, no insult, no disorder. Only resolve.

i18n.hi: हम ताक़त दिखाएँगे संयम से। न पत्थर, न गाली, न हुड़दंग—सिर्फ़ अडिग इरादा।

panels.shot: wide shot of Lahore streets being prepared with banners and armbands
panels.action: On a bustling Lahore street, men and women fix black cloth banners to poles, some tie black arm-bands, shopkeepers pause at their doorways watching as students roll out a cart loaded with placards.

dialogue.text: Black flags were sewn. Slogans were whispered and rehearsed. The city prepared not for riot, but for a show of silent defiance.

i18n.hi: काले झंडे सिले गए। नारे धीरे–धीरे बोले, बार–बार दोहराए गए। शहर ने दंगे नहीं, चुप विरोध की तैयारी की।

panels.shot: wide establishing shot of Lahore railway station exterior
panels.action: Outside Lahore’s colonial railway station, its brick arches and clock tower looming, policemen in khaki stand in small clusters, while distant groups of citizens begin to gather at the edges of the open space.

dialogue.text: On 30 October 1928, the day of the Commission’s arrival, the station became a stage. The government sent its police. The city sent its people.

i18n.hi: 30 अक्तूबर 1928 को, कमीशन के आने के दिन, स्टेशन ही मंच बन गया। सरकार ने पुलिस भेजी, शहर ने अपना जनसमूह।

panels.shot: low-angle wide shot of a marching crowd led by him
panels.action: Lajpat Rai marches at the head of a long, orderly procession of men and women, many holding black flags, their faces solemn, as the broad road toward the station stretches ahead under a hazy sky.

dialogue.text: Lajpat Rai, past sixty and often in poor health, took his place at the front. For him, leadership meant walking first into danger, not watching from behind.

i18n.hi: साठ पार, कमज़ोर सेहत के बावजूद, लाजपत राय सबसे आगे खड़े हुए। उनके लिए नेतागिरी का मतलब था ख़तरे में सबसे पहले कदम रखना, पीछे से देखना नहीं।

panels.shot: mid-shot side view of him walking with cane amid younger supporters
panels.action: In profile, Lajpat Rai walks steadily, one hand lightly touching a cane, young students flanking him, one glancing at him with admiration while others clutch placards to their chests.

dialogue.speaker: lala_lajpat_rai
dialogue.text: Remember, we protest not with hatred, but with a clear demand: our right to be heard.

i18n.hi: याद रखो, हम नफ़रत से नहीं, अपने हक़ से खड़े हैं—हक़ बोलने का।

panels.shot: tight shot of black flags and police lines facing each other
panels.action: A line of black flags held high by protesters faces a tight line of police with bamboo lathis held across their chests, both sides tense, the space between them charged and narrow.

dialogue.text: At the station gates, black flags met khaki uniforms. The protestors chanted against the Commission; the police were ordered to keep the road clear at any cost.

i18n.hi: स्टेशन के फाटक पर काले झंडे, खाकी وردियों से टकराए। नारे कमीशन के ख़िलाफ़ उठे, पुलिस को हर हाल में सड़क खाली रखने का हुक्म मिला।

panels.shot: mid-shot of him addressing the crowd just before confrontation
panels.action: Standing just ahead of the crowd, Lajpat Rai turns slightly back toward them, his hand raised to calm them, while beyond him policemen begin to shift uneasily into formation.

dialogue.speaker: lala_lajpat_rai
dialogue.text: Hold your lines. Let them see our discipline. Let the world judge.

i18n.hi: क़तार मत तोड़ो। उन्हें हमारा अनुशासन दिखाओ। दुनिया को फैसला करने दो।

panels.shot: dynamic side-angle wide shot as police advance with lathis raised
panels.action: From the side, a line of policemen charges forward, lathis lifted, captured mid-stride, as Lajpat Rai and the front ranks brace themselves, faces set, some flinching but holding ground.

dialogue.text: Orders were given. The police advanced. For them, it was a crowd to be broken; for the protestors, a line they would not abandon.

i18n.hi: हुक्म चला। पुलिस बढ़ी। उनके लिए ये तोड़ने लायक भीड़ थी, प्रदर्शनकारियों के लिए छोड़ने लायक नहीं, आख़िरी रेखा।

panels.shot: tight mid-shot focused on Lajpat Rai being struck, framed without injury detail
panels.action: The image freezes as a policeman’s raised bamboo stick comes down toward Lajpat Rai’s shoulders and back; his body bends slightly with the impact, his face tight but unyielding, the crowd blurring around him.

dialogue.text: Blows fell on the front ranks. They fell on Lajpat Rai. The Lion of Punjab felt the weight of the lathis, but he did not turn away.

i18n.hi: डंडे सामने की कतार पर बरसे। लाजपत राय पर भी। पंजाब के शेर पर लाठी चली, पर उन्होंने पीठ नहीं फ़ेरी।

panels.shot: close-up on his face immediately after, eyes fierce yet controlled
panels.action: A close shot captures Lajpat Rai breathing hard, sweat on his brow, jaw clenched, eyes burning with pain and determination as the chaos swirls behind him out of focus.

dialogue.speaker: lala_lajpat_rai
dialogue.text: If they strike us for demanding voice, let the nation remember this day.

i18n.hi: अगर आवाज़ माँगने पर हमें पीटा जाएगा, तो देश इस दिन को याद रखे।

panels.shot: wide shot of dispersing crowd and lingering dust after the clash
panels.action: Dust hangs in the air as the crowd slowly disperses from the station approach, some limping, some supporting one another, while policemen stand scattered, and black flags lie trampled on the ground beside discarded shoes.

dialogue.text: The protest was broken by force. Yet the image of a frail, ageing leader beaten for a peaceful march spread far beyond Lahore’s streets.

i18n.hi: जुलूस ज़ोर से कुचला गया, पर बूढ़े, थके नेता की पिटी हुई, शांत छवि लाहौर की सड़कों से बहुत दूर तक फैल गई।

panels.shot: interior mid-shot of him resting in a simple bed, attended by a doctor
panels.action: In a modest room, Lajpat Rai lies propped on pillows on a simple cot, a thin blanket over him; a doctor sits by his side listening gravely, while a small group of anxious colleagues stands near the doorway.

dialogue.text: In the days that followed, his body bore the hidden damage. He continued his work and meetings, but the blows had gone deeper than anyone wished to admit.

i18n.hi: अगले दिनों में बदन अंदर–ही–अंदर चोटें ढोता रहा। वे मीटिंगें करते रहे, काम भी, पर घाव भीतर तक उतर गए थे, जितना कोई मानना नहीं चाहता था।

panels.shot: gentle close-up of his hand writing a final statement
panels.action: A close view shows Lajpat Rai’s hand, veins prominent, holding a pen that moves slowly across paper on a small desk beside his bed, as an inkpot and folded spectacles rest nearby.

dialogue.text: Even as illness grew, he dictated and wrote, placing on record his protest against a commission that had refused India a seat at its own table.

i18n.hi: बीमारी बढ़ती रही, फिर भी उन्होंने बोलकर, लिखकर दर्ज किया—उस कमीशन के ख़िलाफ़ जो भारत को उसकी ही मेज़ पर जगह देने से मुकर गया था।

panels.shot: somber wide shot of a funeral procession in Lahore streets
panels.action: A long, sorrowful procession winds through Lahore’s streets, men and women walking slowly behind a flower-covered bier, heads bowed, shops half-shuttered, while a cloudy sky hangs low over the city.

dialogue.text: On 17 November 1928, Lala Lajpat Rai died. Many in Punjab and beyond linked his death to the lathi blows, and grief hardened into anger and resolve.

i18n.hi: 17 नवंबर 1928 को लाला लाजपत राय नहीं रहे। पंजाब ही नहीं, दूर–दूर तक लोगों ने उनकी मौत को लाठीचार्ज से जोड़ा—सोग ग़ुस्से और पक्के इरादे में बदल गया।

panels.shot: interior shot of young nationalists reading The Tribune with his obituary
panels.action: In a modest office, young nationalists sit around a table reading an issue of The Tribune spread open, a black-bordered notice of Lajpat Rai’s death visible as they exchange grave, intent looks.

dialogue.text: Newspapers carried reports of the Lahore protest and his passing. From Punjab to the United Provinces, a new edge entered the struggle—a sense that peaceful courage had been answered with batons.

i18n.hi: अख़बारों ने लाहौर के विरोध और उनकी मौत की खबरें छापीं। पंजाब से यूनाइटेड प्रॉविंसेज़ तक जंग की भाषा में नई धार आ गई—ये अहसास कि शांत हिम्मत का जवाब डंडों से दिया गया।

panels.shot: symbolic wide shot of the Simon Commission members in session at a distance
panels.action: In a distant, formal hall, suited members of the Simon Commission sit around a polished table, papers spread out, their expressions bureaucratic and remote, the room lit coldly by large windows.

dialogue.text: The Simon Commission continued its work, touring an India that met it with black flags and boycotts. Its very existence, without Indian members, became a lesson in what Indians would no longer accept.

i18n.hi: साइमन कमीशन अपना दौरा करता रहा, पर हर जगह उसे काले झंडे और बहिष्कार मिले। उसका होना ही, बिना भारतीय सदस्यों के, सबक बन गया कि भारत अब क्या नहीं सहेगा।

panels.shot: semi-symbolic shot with his figure looming over a scene of later protests
panels.action: An ethereal, larger-than-life image of Lajpat Rai, calm and resolute, watches over a scene of later mass protests with men and women marching, spinning wheels and tricolour flags appearing among the crowd.

dialogue.text: Lala Lajpat Rai’s last march did not end at the station gate. It walked on in the minds of those who saw in his calm defiance a path of disciplined protest and unbending self-respect.

i18n.hi: लाला लाजपत राय की आख़िरी रैली स्टेशन फाटक पर नहीं रुकी। उनकी शांत, सख़्त अवहेलना उन सबके मन में चलती रही, जिन्होंने उसमें अनुशासित विरोध और अटूट स्वाभिमान की राह देखी।

title_i18n.en: Lala Lajpat Rai Faces the Simon Commission
title_i18n.hi: लाला लाजपत राय बनाम साइमन कमीशन

subtitle_i18n.en: The Lion of Punjab Stands Against a Voiceless Commission
subtitle_i18n.hi: पंजाब का शेर, गूँगा कमीशन के ख़िलाफ़
